quote.text: Sunlight is said to be the best of disinfectants; electric light the most efficient policeman.
quote.author: Louis D. Brandeis, "What Publicity Can Do," Harper's Weekly (20 Dec 1913), reprinted in Other People's Money, and How the Bankers Use It (1914)

macro.title: What It Means For VWRA, Global Bonds, Gold & BTC

stories.headline: Tehran suspends the peace it signed a month ago
stories.dek: Iran formally walks back the Islamabad MoU as strikes reach a Kuwaiti oil terminal and two more American service members are killed — three capitals, three very different stories about who broke it first.

stories.headline: A whitelist filing becomes eleven criminal charges
stories.dek: The Megaspeed case has moved from investigation to courtroom — the first Nvidia-chip-diversion allegation in Asia with both a prosecutor and a defendant on record.

stories.headline: Sugar, found drifting between the stars
stories.dek: Erythrulose is the first "true sugar" ever detected in interstellar space — and the first find structurally close to what living chemistry actually runs on.

stories.headline: A S$2.128 billion bid, still unsigned
stories.dek: Bayshore Drive drew Singapore's first-ever S$2bn-plus bid for a site outside the CBD. The award, notably, has not actually been made yet.

stories.headline: A Saturday launch clears 54% at $2,350 psf
stories.dek: Lentor Gardens Residences' booking day complicates the "only the centre is strong" story — outer-ring demand isn't dead, it just needed the right precinct.

stories.headline: The Xreal R1 shipped. The reviews agree on one thing.
stories.dek: ASUS ROG's AR gaming glasses have graduated from "delayed" to "reviewed" — and every review lands on the same complaint.

stories.headline: Home Assistant stops thinking in entity IDs
stories.dek: 2026.7's real news for a Lutron migrator is a rebuilt Matter server and automations you can describe in a sentence.

stories.headline: A Rainbow Daytona breaks Australia's watch-auction record
stories.dek: $510,000 hammer, $612,000 all-in — and it's still not the biggest result of the week to come.

stories.headline: Three albums, one Friday
stories.dek: Gracie Abrams, Steve Lacy and Rick Ross all released the same day — and for once, that's the actual story, not a chip-stock echo of last issue's box office.

stories.headline: Roblox's age bands get real teeth
stories.dek: General guidance, not medical or legal advice. A safety policy that used to be mostly paperwork now has a stock-price cost and a state settlement attached.

stories.headline: One oil shock, four different instruments
stories.dek: Market explanation and education, not investment advice. Forecasts are flagged as forecasts throughout.

stories.headline: The divorce rate is at a 50-year low. That's not quite good news.
stories.dek: The number is real. The story it's usually told as — marriages getting happier — probably isn't.

stories.headline: More strength training stops paying off around two hours a week
stories.dek: General guidance, not medical advice. A 30-year, 147,000-person dataset finds a ceiling — not a floor.

stories.headline: BIGBANG finally confirms Singapore
stories.dek: After ten straight issues of silence, a date — though not yet a ticket.

stories.headline: The Cost of Daylight
stories.dek: Three episodes, a century and a half apart, about what actually happens once a quiet rule gets noticed — and one honest case, from this very issue, where noticing wasn't enough.
